non_goals: No DB reads, audits, migrations, DDL, PRAGMAs, repairs, vacuuming, file deletion, or schema initialization.
No route handler, auth, request payload, response payload, or runtime validation behavior changes.
No proof, scanner creation, replay, options-profit, or AI commodity semantics.
No tracked-position SQLite fallback and no promotion of test or legacy stores to browser production storage.
No OpenAPI, JSON Schema, generated TypeScript, Pydantic response_model, or stale-artifact governance replacement.
Does not replace repository, migration, constraint, index, local DB, route, proof, scanner, or replay owner manifests.
scope: Generated readability and drift-check map for route, repository, local DB, artifact, and virtual storage ownership.

hard_rules: Do not mix AI commodity artifacts into browser Trading Desk storage ownership.
stores.label: AI commodity artifacts

local_database_roles.active_scope: Separate AI commodity proof-first lane artifacts.
expected_connection_policy: Owned by AI commodity lane tooling.
local_database_roles.notes: Separate non-browser proof lane; excluded from Trading Desk local DB hardening.

stores.production_role: Separate non-browser proof-first AI commodity lane artifacts.

hard_rules: Do not treat backend/domain as a hidden DB or repository fallback.
stores.label: Backend domain placeholder
notes: Use the route owner module for the concrete implementation path.
stores.production_role: Placeholder for backend-only routes without a concrete route-lifecycle store contract.

hard_rules: Do not model backend tool dispatch as a concrete database.
stores.label: Backend tool dispatch

hard_rules: Forward evidence can support proof but does not make suggested trades production proof rows.
Exact-looking payloads without verified scan lineage remain proof-ineligible.
stores.label: Forward evidence artifacts
stores.production_role: Forward scan evidence, scanner lineage, and lifecycle event readbacks.

hard_rules: Use forward-evidence runbooks rather than Trading Desk DB repair workflows.
stores.label: Forward tracking ledgers

local_database_roles.backup_rule: Use forward-evidence runbooks; do not repair from this Trading Desk audit.
expected_connection_policy: Owned by forward-evidence tooling, not Trading Desk repositories.
local_database_roles.notes: Forward evidence is proof-adjacent but not a local repository hardening target.

stores.production_role: Canonical and archive forward-evidence ledgers outside repository hardening.

hard_rules: Replay artifacts do not redefine scanner policy or proof truth by themselves.
stores.label: Latest replay artifacts
stores.production_role: Strategy Lab replay result, summary, report, comparison, and diagnostics artifacts.

hard_rules: Session status is auth state, not repository or proof storage.
stores.label: Local operator session cookie
stores.production_role: Local browser unlock/session state for operator auth.

hard_rules: Outside Trading Desk repository migrations, constraints, indexes, and local DB repair.
stores.label: Market data cache

local_database_roles.active_scope: Market data cache and support data.
local_database_roles.backup_rule: Use market-data cache runbooks; cache rebuilds are outside Point 15.
expected_connection_policy: Owned by market data cache code, not Trading Desk repositories.
local_database_roles.notes: Mutable cache, but outside Trading Desk repository migrations/constraints/indexes.
local_database_roles.owner: market data service and research support workflows

notes: Browser-readable support storage is not Trading Desk repository storage.
owners: market data service and research support workflows
stores.production_role: Market data cache and support readbacks such as sectors/cache stats.

hard_rules: Do not migrate or repair this store through Trading Desk repository contracts.
stores.label: Options history truth store

local_database_roles.active_scope: Imported options truth store and replay input.
local_database_roles.backup_rule: Use options-history import/replay runbooks; do not repair from this Trading Desk audit.
expected_connection_policy: Owned by options-history/import tooling, not Trading Desk repositories.
local_database_roles.notes: Large proof/research store; Point 15 does not open or mutate it by default.

stores.production_role: Imported options truth store and replay input, outside Trading Desk repository hardening.

hard_rules: Options-profit status consumes proof and repository readbacks; it is not a proof owner.
stores.label: Options-profit state artifacts
stores.production_role: Options-profit status, gates, and proof/profit readbacks.

hard_rules: Tracked positions are Postgres-owned in the browser product.
Missing or failing DATABASE_URL fails closed through the unavailable sentinel.
Do not silently fall back to SQLite.
stores.label: Postgres tracked positions
notes: Proof definitions remain in the proof contract, not in storage ownership.
stores.production_role: Production tracked-position rows, reviews, closes, proof/readback counts, and runtime health.

hard_rules: Do not treat prediction history as Trading Desk repository proof storage.
stores.label: Predictions JSON
stores.production_role: Prediction history and grade route artifact state.

hard_rules: Do not treat sidecars or backups as active stores.
stores.label: SQLite sidecars and backups

local_database_roles.active_scope: SQLite sidecars and backups generated by local runtime or repair scripts.
local_database_roles.backup_rule: Keep ignored locally unless a specific recovery workflow needs them.
expected_connection_policy: Do not treat sidecars or backups as active stores.
local_database_roles.notes: Audit scripts may enumerate these files but must not delete or rewrite them.

stores.production_role: Local sidecars and backups generated by runtime or repair workflows.

hard_rules: Suggested trades remain separate from tracked-position proof rows.
Suggested trades are not broker fills and do not feed production proof truth.
stores.label: SQLite suggested trades

local_database_roles.active_scope: Trading Desk suggested trades and local paper workflow state.
local_database_roles.backup_rule: Create an application-aware SQLite backup before manual mutation; do not prune backups from audit scripts.
expected_connection_policy: repository write connections enable PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON
audits open existing files with SQLite mode=ro and do not initialize schema
local_database_roles.notes: This is the only active mutable Trading Desk SQLite browser store.

notes: Repository write connections enable SQLite foreign keys.
stores.production_role: Local paper and hypothetical suggested-trade workflow state.

hard_rules: Do not route browser tracked-position traffic to this store.
Do not promote this store to production fallback behavior.
stores.label: SQLite tracked positions test/legacy

local_database_roles.active_scope: Explicit tests and legacy tools only; never the browser tracked-position fallback.
local_database_roles.backup_rule: Prefer JSON snapshots or SQLite backup API for legacy repair scripts; do not copy live SQLite files without quiescing writers.
expected_connection_policy: legacy repository connections currently request PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL
audits open existing files with SQLite mode=ro and do not initialize schema
local_database_roles.notes: This DB is local legacy/test state and must not replace Postgres tracked positions.

notes: Kept visible so agents do not mistake legacy SQLite for production tracked storage.
stores.production_role: Explicit tests and legacy tools only; not browser tracked-position fallback.

hard_rules: Profile saves require local operator auth and mutation intent at the route boundary.
stores.label: Strategy profile files
stores.production_role: Strategy Lab profile, risk, and changelog state.

record_boundaries.notes: Route headers and docs must keep the store and record class distinct.
record_boundaries.rule: Never collapse suggested trades into tracked positions or tracked positions into SQLite fallback state.

record_boundaries.notes: Suggested source snapshots can contain scanner context, but suggested rows are not production proof rows.
record_boundaries.tracked: source_scan_session_id, source_scan_event_key, source_scan_run_id, source_scan_recorded_at_utc, proof_eligible, proof_ineligibility_reason, proof_class, proof_class_reason

record_boundaries.notes: Suggested-trade mutations are local paper workflow state and do not write lifecycle proof events.
record_boundaries.rule: Forward-evidence lifecycle event persistence remains tracked-position-only.

record_boundaries.notes: Open paper ideas can be reviewed for operator workflow, not proof-lane claims.
record_boundaries.rule: Suggested trades do not feed proof-summary or options-profit production truth.
record_boundaries.suggested: not required and not production proof/profit input

record_boundaries.notes: This boundary survives even when scanner-origin safety gates are shared.
record_boundaries.rule: Suggested trades are not broker fills, not production proof, and not a tracked-position fallback.
record_boundaries.tracked: production tracked positions and historical learning rows

route_parity.intentional_difference: Tracked readbacks can feed proof/profit summaries; suggested reads are paper ideas only.
route_parity.shared_contract: status filtering, optional paging, grouped all-status reads, and lifecycle headers

route_parity.intentional_difference: Tracked creates persist proof/source-scan fields and lifecycle event status; suggested creates do not.
route_parity.shared_contract: manual/scanner-origin payload building and scanner creation-safety validation

route_parity.intentional_difference: Tracked reviews report position_event_persistence; suggested reviews update paper ideas only.
route_parity.shared_contract: positions_service.review_open_positions computes review payloads for open rows

route_parity.intentional_difference: Suggested close reason is manual_hypothetical_close and never broker/live proof.
route_parity.shared_contract: close rows store exit price, close time, P&L snapshot, and synthetic SELL latest_review
